'''Dominic Peters''' (born 11 December 1978 in [[Acton, London|Acton]]) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer.

Peters played for the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]]. He has also played for the [[London Skolars]]. Dom Peters has also played for [[Wasps RFC|London Wasps]].<ref name="London Wasps squad for tonight's game">{{cite web|url=http://www.wasps.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=8363&newstype=r|publisher=London Wasps|title=London Wasps squad for tonight's game|date=2004-09-19|accessdate=2007-10-19|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20110605081021/http://www.wasps.co.uk/newspage.ink?nid=8363&newstype=r|archivedate=5 June 2011|df=dmy-all}}</ref>

Dominic Peters' position of choice was as a {{rlp|wg}}. He could also operate in the {{rlp|sr}}.

==Early career==
Dom Peters was introduced to Rugby Union at Gunnersbury Catholic School, Brentford, Middlesex.  It was here that he was recognised as a strong and powerful winger, scoring many tries over a few seasons with the 1st team, helping them on their way to win the Middlesex County Cup for three years in a row from 1995–97.

Dom Peters was seen as a shining example for all inner-city children wanting to get into professional sport after coming through the Broncos youth system.<ref name="In a league of his own">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sportacademy/hi/sa/rugby_league/features/newsid_2112000/2112880.stm|title=In a league of his own|publisher=BBC|date=n.d.|accessdate=2007-10-19}}</ref> He was seen as a highly promising winger when injury-free.<ref name="Griffin helps push back boundaries">{{cite news|url=http://sport.independent.co.uk/rugby_league/article108869.ece|title=Griffin helps push back boundaries|publisher=Independent|date=2003-06-15|accessdate=2007-10-19|location=London|first=Dave|last=Hadfield}}{{dead link|date=August 2021|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>

==Drug Controversy==

[[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]] terminated the contract of Dominic Peters after he was handed a 12-month ban by the [[Rugby Football League|RFL]] for taking the banned drug [[stanozolol]].<ref name="Broncos sack Peters">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/2947964.stm|title=Broncos sack Peters|publisher=BBC|date=2003-05-23|accessdate=2007-10-19}}</ref><ref name="Trio fail drugs tests">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/2941968.stm|title=Trio fail drugs tests|publisher=BBC|date=2003-05-27|accessdate=2007-10-19}}</ref><ref name="Peters forced out after drug ban">{{cite web|url=http://www2.newsquest.co.uk/local_london/ealing/sport/SPORT3.html|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20020824222005/http://www2.newsquest.co.uk/local_london/ealing/sport/SPORT3.html|url-status=dead|archive-date=24 August 2002|title=Peters forced out after drug ban|publisher=Ealing News|date=2003-06-05|accessdate=2007-10-19|df=dmy-all}}</ref> Peters had the remaining 12 months of his contract torn up after he was banned for steroid abuse.<ref name="Peters 'devastated' by ban">{{cite news|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/super_league/2950352.stm|title=Peters 'devastated' by ban|publisher=BBC|date=2003-05-30|accessdate=2007-10-19}}</ref>

==Representative Football==
Peters was a [[Lancashire rugby league team|Lancashire Origin]] player, despite being [[London]] born and bred.<ref name="Peters enticed by red rose call">{{cite news|url=http://sport.independent.co.uk/rugby_league/article245339.ece|title=Peters enticed by red rose call|publisher=Independent|date=2001-05-22|accessdate=2007-10-19|location=London|first=Dave|last=Hadfield|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20071121153658/http://sport.independent.co.uk/rugby_league/article245339.ece|archivedate=21 November 2007|df=dmy-all}}</ref><ref name="Lancashire bloom after long wait">{{cite news|url=https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/main.jhtml?xml=/sport/2001/06/05/slyork06.xml|title=Lancashire bloom after long wait|publisher=Telegraph|date=2001-06-05|accessdate=2007-10-19| location=London}}{{dead link|date=July 2021|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>

Peters played in the first and only fixture of [[West Indies national rugby league team|West Indies]] v [[South Africa national rugby league team|South Africa]] in 2004. He played in the {{rlp|sr}} and scored a try.

Dom Peters is a [[Rugby Union]] international with [[Barbados national rugby union team|Barbados]].<ref name="Peters Bajans on the march">{{cite news|url=http://sport.independent.co.uk/rugby_league/article245339.ece|title=Bajans on the march|publisher=Telegraph|date=2005-10-05|accessdate=2007-10-19|location=London|first=Dave|last=Hadfield|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20071121153658/http://sport.independent.co.uk/rugby_league/article245339.ece|archivedate=21 November 2007|df=dmy-all}}</ref>
